subject: UX & Research
title: The Science of Categories
description: A quick win: understand The Science of Categories.

hook.text: Is a tomato a fruit or a vegetable? Botanically it's a fruit. Culinarily it's a vegetable. Categories depend on context.

buildup.text: Eleanor Rosch showed in the 1970s that categories have fuzzy boundaries. A robin is more 'bird' than a penguin.

discovery.text: Prototypes define categories: the most typical example sets the mental standard. Chairs have legs and a back.

twist.text: Users categorize differently than designers. 'Settings' feels like 'account' to some, 'preferences' to others.

climax.text: Test your categories with card sorting. Users reveal where your labels match or clash with their mental models.

punchline.text: Categories are fuzzy. Test them with real users, not internal opinions.

quiz.question: What did Eleanor Rosch discover about human categorization?
options: Categories have fuzzy boundaries with more and less typical members
Humans organize everything alphabetically by default
Categories are identical across all cultures
